Kawayan Renewables, Inc. (SEC Reg. No. CS2021-091234; TIN 296-123-456-000) submits this incident report under SRC Rule 17.1.
On 28 May 2026, a Business Email Compromise exposed an HR mailbox and a Treasury shared drive in our San Palito City, Barangay Mapagkaisa office.
Two files contained employee PII: Mr. Ramon J. Ilagan (TIN 345-987-123-000; SSS 34-1234567-9; Passport No. P1234567F) and Ms. Liza R. Bautista (TIN 458-321-765-000; SSS 45-7654321-8; Passport No. P7654321B).
The compromised archive also listed Treasury settlement details: Talulot Savings Bank Current Account No. 012345678901 and a vendor routing code 000-000-000 (placeholder).
An invalid identifier in a template (“SSS No.: __________”, “TIN 000-000-000-000”) and a test record SSS 11-1111111-1 (invalid checksum) were observed, none containing live data.
Contact details exposed included the HR desk line +63 917 802 3344 and Mr. Ilagan’s work email ramon.ilagan@kawayanrenewables.ph; the number 1800-000-0000 is a public helpline for building security.
Separately, a draft, non-public term sheet for a 20 MW rooftop PPA and preliminary Q2 EBITDA guidance were in the same mailbox; the trading window has been closed per insider-trading controls.
No customer bank card data was stored, and no biometrics were involved.
We have notified the National Privacy Commission under RA 10173 and the SEC pursuant to SRC Rule 17 and PSE disclosure procedures.
Immediate actions: password resets, MFA enforcement, vendor token revocation, and suspension of the role account “HR Manager” pending review.
We confirm that payroll tax filings (BIR Form 1601-C) were unaffected and that the branch at Unit 1203, Luntian Tower, 88 Makisig St., Barangay Malikhain, San Palito City 0999 remains operational.
For queries, contact Mr. Paulo D. Alonzo, Data Protection Officer, at dpo@kawayanrenewables.ph and +63 2 8123 4001; for investors, ir@kawayanrenewables.ph.
This disclosure excludes granular projections and counterparty names; any material updates will be released via the PSE platform in due course.
